steps.title: Translate the phrase into an expression
steps.content: "12 less than 6 times $n$" means $6n - 12$. This is the minimum value of $x$.

steps.title: Write the inequality
steps.content: If $6n - 12$ is the minimum value of $x$, then $x$ must be greater than or equal to it: $x \ge 6n - 12$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• A. $x \le 6n - 12$ — this would make $6n - 12$ the maximum, not the minimum; incorrect.
  • B. $x \ge 6n - 12$ — matches our derivation.
  • C. $x \le 12 - 6n$ — wrong expression and wrong direction; incorrect.
  • D. $x \ge 12 - 6n$ — the expression $12 - 6n$ reverses the phrase; incorrect.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
